items.title: De Roon adoreert Nico Williams: 'Hij heeft ons verliefd laten worden op Spanje'
items.summary: In aanloop naar de Champions League-ontmoeting tussen Athletic Bilbao en Atalanta heeft Marten de Roon zijn bewondering voor Nico Williams uitgesproken. Dankzij de vleugelflitser werd De Roon zelfs een beetje verliefd op het Spaanse elftal tijdens het afgelopen EK.

items.title: VI Live: Ajax naast Villarreal dankzij geschutter bij vrije trap Gloukh
items.summary: Een heerlijk begin van de tweede helft bij Villarreal - Ajax. De thuisploeg kwam al snel op voorsprong dankzij een fraaie knal van Tani Olwaseyi. Oscar Gloukh zette de Amsterdammers langszij door een vrije trap raak te schieten: 1-1. Ajax hoopt de kansen op de tussenronde van de Champions League in leven te houden. Mis niks via dit liveblog. We volgen in een andere VI Live de ontwikkelingen bij de andere Champions League-duels van dinsdagavond .

items.title: VI Live: Real stevent af op monsterscore, Arsenal op voorsprong in Milaan
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van dinsdag 20 januari. De Champions League gaat na een korte winterstop weer verder! We houden je hier op de hoogte van de ontwikkelingen. De VI Live van Villarreal - Ajax vind je hier .

items.title: Ronaldo wins long-running Juve legal dispute
items.summary: Juventus have lost an appeal to overturn a court ruling that had awarded their former striker Cristiano Ronaldo millions in owed wages, the Serie A club confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Slot 'happy' to welcome Salah back at Liverpool
items.summary: Arne Slot welcomed Mohamed Salah's return to Liverpool's squad and dropped a major hint the Egyptian forward would start against Marseille.

items.title: Sources: Fulham in talks to sign Man City's Bobb
items.summary: Fulham are in talks with Manchester City over a move for winger Oscar Bobb, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: 10-man Man City stunned by tiny Bodø/Glimnt
items.summary: Manchester City slumped to one of the most humiliating upsets in Champions League history, losing to a 3-1 at tiny Norwegian club Bodø/Glimt on Tuesday.

items.title: Ter Stegen vindt met oog op WK oplossing voor uitzichtloze situatie bij Barça
items.summary: Marc-André ter Stegen maakt het seizoen af bij Girona. De doelman wordt voor de rest van het seizoen gehuurd van streekgenoot Barcelona, zo kondigde trainer Hansi Flick dinsdag aan.

items.title: Zoon van Priske tekent in Engeland, Bailey terug naar Villa
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Slot hints Salah will start for Liverpool in Marseille
items.summary: Arne Slot suggests Mohamed Salah will return to the Liverpool starting line-up against Marseille in Wednesday's Champions League tie, insisting his relationship with the forward is "as normal as it's always been".

items.title: Bodø/Glimt give Manchester City one hell of a Champions League beating
items.summary: To channel Bjorge Lillelien and his famous commentary on Norway’s win against England in 1981: Pep Guardiola, your Manchester City boys took a heck of a beating here on the shores of the Norwegian Sea, below the skies of the aurora borealis, and on the Aspmyra Stadion’s artificial pitch graced by this immortal Bodø/Glimt victory which downed a continental superpower. Jonas Gahr Støre was present to witness a win that came courtesy of Kasper Høgh’s two first-half goals plus Jens Petter Hauge’s curled peach after the interval, as Norway’s prime minister escaped Donald Trump’s curious obsession with the Nobel peace prize: another measure of how this result will never be forgotten. Continue reading...

items.title: Concerned European football chiefs discuss response to Trump over Greenland
items.summary: Annex attempt could bring about Uefa-led boycott Implications for World Cup alarming heads of FAs European football leaders are increasingly concerned about Donald Trump’s wish to annex Greenland, and they have held initial discussions about how the sport could respond. The Guardian understands the implications for the World Cup this summer were among the topics raised among about 20 football association heads in Budapest on Monday. Talks about the Greenland crisis were held informally on the sidelines of an event organised to celebrate the Hungarian football federation’s 150th anniversary, in the knowledge that a unified European response may be required should Trump seek to escalate the situation. Continue reading...

items.title: From bad to worse: Now City suffer shock defeat at Bodo
items.summary: Manchester City were left stunned as their miserable 2026 continued after Bodo/Glimt produced a scintillating attacking performance to secure a shock 3-1 Champions League victory.

items.title: Ipswich, Coventry lead; Norwich thumping WBA | Championship on Sky LIVE!
Rosenior adamant PSG and Real Madrid target Fernández’s long-term future is at Chelsea
items.summary: Fernández linked with Champions League winners Chelsea have no intention of selling Argentina midfielder Liam Rosenior has dealt a blow to Paris Saint-Germain’s and Real Madrid’s hopes of signing Enzo Fernández by insisting the midfielder’s long-term future lies at Chelsea. There has been plenty of noise around Fernández over the past week, with reports in France claiming PSG are targeting him as part of their plans to rebuild their midfield this summer. Madrid are also being talked up as a potential destination for the Argentina international, particularly as they are short of a player capable of dictating play, but Rosenior has no doubts about the 25-year-old’s commitment to Chelsea. Continue reading...

items.title: Refs' boss Webb backs officials over Dalot incident
items.summary: Premier League referees' boss Howard Webb rejects claims that his officials made a mistake by not sending off Diogo Dalot in Saturday's Manchester derby.

items.title: Return of the 0-0 - is the Premier League getting boring?
items.summary: Two goalless fixtures at the weekend mean this season's Premier League has already featured more 0-0 draws than the whole of last term – but does that mean the league has been boring?
